You may not have liked the choices they made but it was definitely not rushed. They finished filming it January 2018 and did a few reshoot sessions in late 2019 but that's pretty normal for a movie of that scale. They thought out what they did clearly and painstakingly, but their choices are going to polarize some fans regardless. I am personally medium on science Hulk but I loved chunky Thor and that it didn't hinder his fighting at all just his mental state, and yeah characters were gonna die, that was always gonna happen.

When do I get a spoiler pass?

Three weeks after Thanos used the Infinity Stones to disintegrate half of all life across the universe,[N 1] Carol Danvers rescues Nebula and Tony Stark from deep space. They return to Earth and join the remaining Avengers—Natasha Romanoff, Bruce Banner, Steve Rogers, Rocket, Thor, and James Rhodes. Using Nebula's knowledge, they find an injured Thanos on an unguarded planet and intend to retake the Stones to reverse his actions but find he has already destroyed them to prevent further use. An enraged Thor beheads Thanos.

Five years later, Scott Lang escapes the quantum realm[N 2] and meets Romanoff and Rogers at the Avengers' compound. Lang explains living through a duration of five hours instead of five years, theorizing that the quantum realm allows time travel. The three ask Stark to help them retrieve the Stones from the past so they can reverse Thanos' actions in the present, but he refuses out of fear of losing his daughter, Morgan. After talking with his wife, Pepper Potts, Stark relents and works with Banner—who has merged his intelligence with the Hulk's body—to stabilize travel in the quantum realm. Banner and Rocket go to the Asgardian refugees' new home in Norway, New Asgard, to recruit a drunken, overweight Thor; Romanoff heads to Tokyo to recruit Clint Barton, now a ruthless vigilante following the disintegration of his family.

Banner, Rogers, Lang, and Stark travel to New York City in 2012.[N 3] Banner visits the Sanctum Sanctorum and convinces the Ancient One to give him the Time Stone, promising to later return the stones to their original place. Rogers successfully retrieves the Mind Stone, but Lang and Stark fail to retrieve the Space Stone after Loki escapes with it. Rogers and Stark travel to S.H.I.E.L.D. headquarters in 1970 and steal an earlier version of the Space Stone as well as vials of Pym Particles from Hank Pym, then return to the present.

Rocket and Thor travel to Asgard in 2013 to retrieve the Reality Stone from Jane Foster,[N 4] and Thor obtains his former hammer, Mjolnir. On Morag in 2014, Nebula and Rhodes steal the Power Stone before Peter Quill can.[N 5] Rhodes returns to the present with the Power Stone while Nebula is unable to return due to her cybernetic implants interfacing with those of her 2014 self. Through this connection, the Thanos of that time learns of his future success and the Avengers' attempts to undo it. Thanos captures present-day Nebula and sends 2014 Nebula to the present. Barton and Romanoff travel to Vormir in the same year and learn from the Soul Stone's keeper, the Red Skull, that it can only be acquired by sacrificing someone they love. Romanoff sacrifices herself, allowing Barton to claim the stone.

After retrieving all of the Infinity Stones, The Avengers create a gauntlet to hold them, which Banner uses to restore those whom Thanos disintegrated by snapping his fingers. 2014 Nebula uses the time machine to transport Thanos to the present, and he attacks the Avengers' compound, planning to remake the universe from scratch with the Stones. Present-day Nebula convinces the 2014 version of her sister, Gamora, to turn against Thanos, and kills the 2014 version of herself. A restored Stephen Strange arrives with his fellow sorcerers, the restored Avengers and Guardians of the Galaxy, as well as the armies of Wakanda, Asgard, and the Ravagers. They fight Thanos' army alongside Danvers, but Thanos seizes the Stones after he overpowers them all. Stark steals the Stones back and disintegrates Thanos and his army. Mortally wounded from the energy the Stones release, Stark dies.

Following Stark's funeral, Thor appoints Valkyrie as the king[5] of New Asgard and joins the Guardians of the Galaxy; Quill begins searching for 2014 Gamora. Rogers returns the Infinity Stones and Mjolnir to their original places in time, then chooses to remain in the past to marry Peggy Carter and live out the rest of his life. In the present, an elderly Rogers passes on his shield to Sam Wilson, while remembering the dance he promised Carter.

There’s something I don’t get. If Strange was able to go to the future to see alternative outcomes of the war it means what he does in past shapes different futures. So how is it not possible for the Avengers to go back in time and kill Thanos before the big snap to prevent all this from happening?

If the 2018 Avengers had gone back in time and killed Thanos before the Snap, then that specific moment's reality/timeline would have changed into a new, separate reality.  The Avengers' original reality would still exist.   

Dr. Strange's plan, the one outcome that had the Avengers winning, was to let the Snap happen, get the Stones after the fact, and then bring everyone back from the dead - in THEIR reality.

Strange can see differente futures and outcomes, but he doesn't intervenes, you can see yourself, when Tony asked about the future, if they can win, Strange knew that Tony was about to die, but he didn't want to intervene in it, it's part of the future and shoulnd't be changed. He only sees the future, can't travel to it.

@gasparmxm ... Strange "won't" travel to the future.  He has the ability, but he knows that the risks are too great.   

Indeed, Strange knew that Stark had to die in order for them to win.  That was the one successful outcome that he saw.  Like he told Stark in Infinity War, he would not hesitate to sacrifice him or Parker in order to save the Universe.

Jose Francisco Mercado .... Superheroes became all the rage long before the 80’s.  The Golden Age of comic book superheroes was the 1960’s. @kristianehussenmaratas6931 ....I am in my late 50's, and my childhood was filled with DC and Marvel comic books. Ayyy i watched the movie today stop reading now if you haven't watched it. PLEASE READ TILL THE END AND CORRECT ME IF I AM WRONG. So see how in Infinity War when Thanos uses the time stone to reverse time and bring Vision back before he was killed but time didn't actually go back just Visions time went back before he died and everything else stayed the same. Couldn't the Avengers do the same for Iron Man and reverse only his time as Thanos showed us in Infinity War that you can reverse someone's time but nothing else changes just their time. So could they have saved Iron Man?

I see it this way:  Vision was just kneeling on the ground, not doing anything, when Wanda destroyed the Mind Stone (and him with it).   Reversing Vision's personal timeline didn't affect anything.  He was once again kneeling on the ground.   

Tony Stark's last act was stripping the Stones off the Gauntlet and then using them to Snap Thanos and his armies out of existence.  All in one act, he defeated Thanos and gave up his life.  

If Dr. Strange had used the Time Stone to reverse Tony Stark's personal timeline, then Stark wouldn't have been there to strip the Stones from Thanos.  They would have lost for good, as Thanos had the Stones and was about to destroy the ENTIRE Universe.

@Stogie2112 I was thinking they didn't have to go too far back in time but just after Tony Stark  took the stones off Thanos because once again it won't change what happened, everything stays the same as he already snapped. So the Stones will stay with Tony Stark and only his time will go back just before he gets hurt like Vision but it doesn't change what happens to everyone else. I mean if you base it on what Infinity War portrayed the time stone to do in the Vision scene i think Stark could have been saved. ONCE AGAIN CORRECT ME IF I AM WRONG.

I like the movie. But here is the honest comment about what it means by power (spoiler? might be...):

1) a) In previous film, Scarlet can pushes back Thanos meters away when he already had 5 stones.
     b) In the current film, Scarlet literally can kill Thanos who does not have any stone yet in 1 vs 1 combat.
=> So my question is: why she just flies around and kills minions? Why don't just use her mind power or "raw power", whatever it is, to confront and kill him?

2) a) Captain Marvel can hold back Thanos who now has full 6 stones.
     b) Captain Marvel takes the gauntlets towards the machine, back to where the stones come from.
     c) Thanos destroys the machine leading someone else has to take it and snaps the finger.
=> Is it so meaningless??? Why does Captain Marvel not just confront Thanos and kills him? Or, when she is holding the gauntlet, as a super-being (more powerful than Thanos), she can use and easily snaps her finger and kills the entire enemies. Why the hell she has to fly to the machine? Why does she not just use the damned gauntlet? Or even without the gauntlet, she alone can wipe out civilizations.

3) I guess the mistake of this film is to try to bring everything into one place, hence a mess. Because this is the final film with high budget and epic visuality and actions, people easily discount those plot-holes. However, in terms of power, I think it is completely meaningless for the actions of Captain Marvel and Scarlet. Their powers are strongly nerfed by giving ways to other teary and touching moments, which, I think, are not that effective.

This thing about ENDGAME has been bugging me for a while. So Thanos in 2014 captures Nebula from the future, learns about the future and travels to 2024(5 years after infinity war) then dies. This is where it gets interesting, whole end game is based on the theory that another timeline won't be created if you replace the things you take from the past, as explained by the ancient one to Banner. But Thanos jumps like 10 years, and dies this means in the timeline there is no Thanos after the point he travels forward in time with his army, if he doesn't return back then who caused the infinity war, if he was not present in the timeline at the time of infinity war, there should be no infinity war hence no endgame is possible, time travel paradox right there.

My birthday is on May 2nd, Thursday, and I know that this will be the best gift. Watching as the Avengers defeat Thanos and getting to see my favorite heroes of my life, even if they are only on the screen, come back better than ever. The only thing I felt was not ok was when Tony died and a great hero with him. He sacrificed himself to save billions, and the whole world of Marvel will know what he did. I love him and everyone hero in that movie. Once again I would like to thank Marvel, Stan Lee, and everyone that helped make the best movies. I know this was long but this means so much to me.

Spoiler Warning




Amazing ending. The whole movie had surprises. Every scene. So many emotions.
Hawkeye’s family disintegrating. When the Avengers give up in the beginning because the stones were destroyed. Hulk when he was half Bruce Banner half Hulk. Iron Man with his daughter. The fat Thor. Black Widow’s death. And when Tony met his father. Captain America fighting his younger self. The big fight with THANOS. Captain America use Mijonir. Every character coming to fight. Iron Man’s death and the record message. And when Captain America stays back to live with Peggy and gives his shield to Sam. It was a amazing story. I think the reason they didn’t give a post credit scene because it was the end of the story. There was no plot twist or new villain. This era of the MCU has come to an end. It is like the last chapter of the any series. This was an amazing ten years. From Iron Man to Avengers Endgame. And I am gonna miss the old avengers. But am excited for a new story to begin.
Thank you for reading.

Three weeks after Thanos uses the Infinity Stones to eliminate half of all life across the Universe,[N 1] Carol Danvers rescues Nebula and Tony Stark from deep space and returns them to Earth. They rejoin the remaining Avengers: Natasha Romanoff, Bruce Banner, Steve Rogers, Rocket, Thor, and James Rhodes. Romanoff explains that world governments are in a state of chaos and trying to conduct censuses to assess the loss of population, as Thanos killed randomly. The team track Thanos to an uninhabited planet he has fled to. Thanos reveals he destroyed the Infinity Stones to prevent the culling from being reversed and is decapitated by Thor.

Five years later, Scott Lang escapes the quantum realm[N 2] and finds Romanoff and Rogers at the Avengers' compound. He suggests traveling back in time via the quantum realm to thwart Thanos in the past. The trio turns to Stark to develop a time machine for them; Stark, now raising a young daughter, only agrees after reflecting on the loss of Peter Parker. Stark assists Banner, who has learned to merge his intelligence with the Hulk, in completing the project. Romanoff recruits Clint Barton, who has become a vigilante after his family was disintegrated. Banner and Rocket find Thor in Norway where he has become a drunken recluse.

The Avengers split into groups to retrieve the Infinity Stones from the past. Banner, Lang, Rogers, and Stark travel to New York City in 2012.[N 3] Banner visits the Sanctum Sanctorum and convinces the Ancient One to give him the Time Stone, promising to return it afterwards to prevent the Universe from breaking down. Meanwhile, Rogers manages to steal the Mind Stone, but Lang and Stark's attempt to steal the Space Stone fails and Loki escapes with it. Rogers and Stark use the last of their size-altering Pym Particles to travel to S.H.I.E.L.D.'s secret base under Camp Lehigh in 1970. They steal the Space Stone and some vials of Pym Particles to return to the present.

Rocket and Thor travel to Asgard in 2013 to retrieve the Reality Stone from Jane Foster,[N 4] where Thor obtains his former hammer, Mjolnir. Barton and Romanoff travel to Vormir in 2014 and learn from the Soul Stone's keeper, the Red Skull, that it can only be acquired by sacrificing someone they love. After a fight between the pair, Romanoff sacrifices herself. On Morag, in the same year, Nebula and Rhodes steal the Power Stone before Peter Quill can.[N 5] Rhodes returns to the present with the Stone, but Nebula is left behind when her cybernetic implants overload as her mind connects with her past self. Thanos uses this connection to access future-Nebula's memories and thereby learns of his future fate and the Avengers' plans. He captures future-Nebula and sends past-Nebula forward to the present as a spy.

Back in the present, Banner and Thor construct a new Infinity Gauntlet, which Banner uses to restore everyone that Thanos disintegrated. Meanwhile, past-Nebula uses the time machine to transport Thanos and his ship to the present day. Thanos obliterates the Avengers' headquarters, aiming to retrieve the Stones and destroy the universe to create a new one in his image. Thanos and his army overpower the Avengers, until Stephen Strange arrives with reinforcements, which include his fellow sorcerers, the resurrected Avengers, the Guardians of the Galaxy, Pepper Potts, and the armies of Asgard, Wakanda, and the Ravagers. Danvers eventually joins the battle and destroys Thanos' ship. Future-Nebula convinces the past version of her sister, Gamora, to betray Thanos, and kills past-Nebula. Thanos retrieves the Gauntlet, but Stark takes the Stones and uses them to disintegrate Thanos and his army. The energy mortally wounds Stark and he dies after saying goodbye to Rhodes, Parker, and Potts.

Following a funeral for Stark, the Avengers disband. Thor appoints Valkyrie as the queen of New Asgard and joins the Guardians of the Galaxy, where Quill is determined to search for Gamora. Rogers returns the Infinity Stones and Mjolnir to their original places in time. He chooses to remain in the past and marry Peggy Carter. In the present, an elderly Rogers passes on his shield to Sam Wilson.

- Tony now has a family and is living in the woods peacefully and refuses to help with time travel

- Rhodey and Nebula Travel to 2014 to steal the Power Stone before the 2014 Quill gets there. - Tony has his own version of the gauntlet and they hold a meeting to who should do the snap. Banner was the best choice since the snap nearly killed Thanos. Banner was their best choice so Banner snaps and injures him in the process. It worked and brought everyone who died back. 
- While all of that was going on the 2014 Nebula manipulates the time machine and uses it to travel Thanos and his army to the current time and succeeds. 
- Thanos attacks the Avengers HQ and reduces it to rubble. Clint retrieves the gauntlet. 
- Thanos waits for Nebula to retrieve the gauntlet. Thor, Tony, and Steve spots Thanos and fights him. Clint being chased by Chitauri and meets Nebula only to find out that was the 2014 Nebula and attempts to swipe the gauntlet only to be killed by the Current Nebula and Gamora. 
- Epic fight occurs. Thanos pins down Thor with Storm breaker almsot killing him only to get knocked out by Mjolnir wielded by Steve.

- The Avenger's keeps the gauntlet away from Thanos but Thanos manages to get the gauntlet but not without Captain Marvel still fighting Thanos to prevent him from snapping. Thanos wins. Strange signals Tony and Tony attempts to fight Thanos for the gauntlet. Thanos wins the fight and snaps his fingers. 
- Nothing Happens and fins out that the stones are not on the gauntlet, Tony has the stones fitted on his hands (his armor) and snaps. Thanos and his army gets dusted. Tony dies from the injury of the stones.

As with many superhero movies, while violence is the main means of conflict resolution, this is primarily a story about courage, self-sacrifice, heroism, and the collective good. Themes also include teamwork, perseverance, and courage. The central characters are willing to die (and kill) to save the universe and rescue the Earth from the grief and trauma they've experienced. The movie also shows how important family and friendship are, and how the concept of family is more than pure biology: people can have incredibly strong chosen families, with friends who love you unconditionally. Emphasizes the idea of being who you are, not who you think you're supposed to be.

Hawkeye's Family dusted by the snap
Captain Marvel saves Tony and Nebula in space
Avengers (along with Captain Marvel) killed Thanos (who used the stones to destroy stones)
5 Years later
Ant-man got out in the Quantum Realm for 5hrs
Ant-man tells Steve and Nat about it
Tony Stark has a daughter
They told Tony about Ant-man, he refuses to help
Steve, Nat and Scott seek for Bruce Banner
Bruce Banner has the body of Hulk with his conciousnes intact
Bruce help them build a Time Machine
Tony help the Avengers in building a Time Machine
Rocket and Bruce recruits Thor (who got Fat and is emotionally unstable). Thor joins
They plan to borrow the Infinity Stones to undo the snaps
They split into 3 Groups
• Steve, Tony, Scott, and Banner in Battle in New York in 2012 to retrieve the Space, Mind and Time Stone
• Nat, WarMachine, Nebula, and Hawkeye in the space to retrieve the Power and Soul Stone (GoTG Vol.1)
• Thor and Rocket in Asgard to retrieve the Reality Stone (Thor: The Dark World)
Steve and Banner successfully retrieve the Mind and Time Stone while Scott and Tony failed to get the Space stone that Loki took.
WarMachine and Nebula got the Power stone but unfortunately Nebula failed to get back due to other Nebula present in that time.
Nat and Clint fight over on who to be the sacrifice, and Nat sacrificed herself to get the Soul Stone. (RIP BlackWidow)
Due to the failure to retrieve the Space Stone, Tony and Steve went back in 1970 to get the Space Stone and to get a Pym Particles to be used in Travelling in Time.
Post-Nebula got capture by Thanos and was beaten up by pre-Nebula 
Pre-Nebula pretends to be post-Nebula to Travel the pre-Thanos in the future to destroy the whole universe
Avengers successfully borrowed all Infinity Stones and Hulk manage to undo the snap that post-Thanos did
Pre-Thanos manage to travel in the future with the help of pre-Nebula
Pre-Thanos destroys the Avengers Headquarters and fights Thor, Tony, and Steve
Steve manage to lift the Mjolhnir and fought Thanos one on one
In the brink of losing, Steve still wants to fight Thanos and his army
Dr. Strange along with other sorcerers brings the other members who got snapped and fight pre-Thanos and his army
Epic Battle occurs
Tony having no choice, used the Infinty Stones to do the snap on pre-Thanos and his army, in exchange for his life (RIP Tony Stark)
Finally a peaceful life and a memorial for Tony Stark
Steve Rogers went back in time to return the Mjolhnir and the Infinity Stones to their respective timeline.
Steve stayed in the past to be with Peggy Carter
Steve became old and gave the shield to Falcon
Thor resigns as a King of Asgard and took Valkyrie as the new Ruler of Asgard
Thor temporary joins the (As)Guardians of the Galaxy

I kind of wish I'd started at the beginning Of course I was only seven when Iron Man came out. I only got into Marvel with Winter Soldier back in 2013. Then Infinity War came out and the trailers were amazing and I was all this is going to be awesome. I wasn't as hyped for Endgame because I felt there were so many was they could screw it up and the  trailers did nothing to put me at ease but it ended up being the most rewarding movie experience iI've ever had. To see all these character arcs wrapped up so beautifully it truly was a historic moment in cinematic history and I'm glad I boarded the hype train early enough to be part of the build up to it.

WARNING ⚠️⚠️⚠️ SPOILERS BELOW 
Please do not read if you haven’t watched the movie but here are my 15 reasons why avengers Infinity war > Avengers Endgame 


1. Thanos shouldn’t have been found so fast. 
2. Professor Hulk was super weak his last fight screen was with thanos in infinity war and did not engage any battle. Nor did he engage with Romanov
3. Thor stayed fat/hobo looking the whole movie. And to think he would make a comeback after speaking with his mother. Sadly no
4. I thought Hulk would have been driven to kill thanos since the soul stone killed his lover aka Romanov(nope)
5. Movie dragged with excessive amounts of talking and too much junk trying to be fitted in that wasn’t necessarily needed
6. Tony Stark shouldn’t have died/ captain American should have
7. Captain America should have went back and brought tony back to life. But too stubborn to do that
8. Captain America lifting Thor’s hammer utterly insane..
9. Captain marvel just should not have been in this film but of course Disney pushed for her but and the Russo brothers had no choice
10. Not one proper send off (cameo) for Stan Lee

My thoughts after seeing it yesterday and letting it have some time to digest:

• My favourite part honestly had to be seeing Pepper as Rescue finally. She was so badass in her suit, like goddamn. 
• I loved the small part Loki played, especially when he imitated Cap again. Also I feel like him disappearing with the Tesseract was intentionally left vague. Could be what his TV series will be about? That would be dope. 
• Didn't think I'd be so upset to see Nat die.
• I thought that BannerHulk's CGI was a little uncanny valley at times.
• Seeing Cap wield Mjolnir was absolutely epic and made me so giddy.
• My display of Loki and Iron Man figurines has sadly become a memorial shrine overnight.
• Honestly didn't think they'd off Tony, but after seeing the movie, it's only fitting to end it with the man who started it all. 
• I'm sad about Tony's death, but also very satisfied. He had a great arc and a very fitting end, and the sendoff was beautiful. 
• Proof that Tony Stark has a heart. Goddamn.
• "And I..... am..... Iron Man" Goddamn. 
• Thor making peace with his mother and getting to see her one last time was awesome.
• The tribute to the original six in the credits was beautifully done. I'll miss these guys so much.

Everything in my life was going well. I had a loving family, had no financial woes, and enjoyed a quiet and fulfilling middle-class American life. But everything changed after I saw the Endgame spoilers. They popped up on my facebook feed out of nowhere. Immediately I knew that this was the end. I could no longer enjoy this film I had been working on enjoying for 11 years the way the Russos intended. All this hard work from going to the theater in 2008 and seeing Robert Downey Jr. put up his suit as Iron Man to crying while his apprentice died in his arms was now dusted away like my favorite heroes. 

I knew I couldn't leave such injustice unpunished. I doxxed my facebook friend from 8th grade that posted the spoilery footage, went to his house, took all of his pokemon cards and burned them in my attic. It should teach him and all the other monsters who ruin precious cinematic experiences a lesson. 

You don't mess with my Marvel movies. You don't spoil one-liners for me. Or I will come after you - and I will avenge my moviegoing experience. 

I'm not sure how I will move on after this. Knowing I will never have seen Avengers: Endgame the way the Russos, Kevin Feige and all the thousands of good men and women who worked on the film wanted me too. I thought about therapy, but then realized I couldn't afford it. I thought about bashing my head against a wall to forget the spoilers I saw, but then I realized that my insurance doesn't cover that neither. 

I am now doomed to live a life of never seeing Avengers: Endgame the way it was meant to be seen. I am writing this so that you may take measures to protect yourself against those spoilers and so that you may never live the life I now have to live.

Following Thanos's activation of the Infinity Gauntlet,[N 1] half of all life across the universe disintegrates, including Clint Barton's family. Meanwhile, Carol Danvers rescues Nebulaand Tony Stark, who are stranded in space following their defeat by Thanos. She returns them on Earth with Natasha Romanoff, Bruce Banner, Steve Rogers, Rocket, Thor, Pepper Potts and James Rhodes. The team tries to retrieve the Infinity Stones from Thanos to reverse his actions, but find he has destroyed them to prevent any further use. Thanos is decapitated by Thor.

Five years later, Banner has merged with the Hulk and Thor has become the drunken ruler of Asgard's refugees in Tønsberg, Norway. Scott Lang escapes from the quantum realm[N 2] and finds Romanoff and Rogers at the Avengers' compound, theorizing that the quantum realm can allow time travel. The three propose to Stark—now raising a daughter, Morgan, with Pepper—that they travel back in time to retrieve the Infinity Stones before Thanos. Stark initially rejects out of concern for Morgan, but relents after reflecting upon the loss of Peter Parker and designs a working time machine.

The reunited Avengers split into groups for their mission. Banner, Rogers, Lang, and Stark travel to the Battle of New York[N 3] to retrieve the Time, Mind, and Space Stones. Banner visits the Sanctum Sanctorum and convinces the Ancient One to give him the Time Stone, and Rogers overcomes undercover Hydraagents and his past self to retrieve the Mind Stone, but Lang and Stark's failed distraction enables Loki to escape with the Space Stone. Rogers and Stark travel back further to the U.S. Army's Fort Leigh in 1970,[N 4] to steal both an earlier version of the Space Stone and vials of Hank Pym's size-altering Pym Particles to return home afterwards. The pair succeed, while meeting Peggy Carter and Howard Stark en route.

In Asgard, Rocket and Thor retrieve the Reality Stone from Jane Foster.[N 5] Thor encounters his mother Frigga, whose counsel restores his conviction, and obtains a past version of his hammer, Mjolnir.[N 6] Barton and Romanoff arrive on Vormir for the Soul Stone. They learn from its keeper, Johann Schmidt, that it can only be retrieved by sacrificing someone they love. After a struggle between the two, Romanoff sacrifices herself.

On Morag, Nebula and Rhodes steal the Power Stone before Peter Quill.[N 7] Rhodes returns it to the present, but Nebula is left stranded behind as her cybernetic implants interface with those of her past self, which Thanos exploits and learns of present-day events. He sends the past incarnation of Nebula to the present in Nebula's place.

Banner activates the Infinity Stones to resurrect everyone killed by Thanos, while Nebula's past self uses the time machine to transport Thanos and his army from the past to the Avengers' facility. They reduce it to rubble, and Barton recovers the Infinity Stones in the chaos. The past Nebula is killed by her present-day self, while Rogers, Thor, and Stark confront Thanos, now planning to use the Infinity Stones to destroy the universe and create a new one with no memory of the original. Rogers proves able to lift Thor's hammer, while Doctor Strange and Wongarrive and teleport all of the revived Avengers and others, including the armies of Wakandaand Asgard, to battle Thanos. Stark eventually retrieves and activates the Infinity Stones, which disintegrates Thanos and his army, though Stark himself perishes in the process.

Following a funeral for Stark, Thor appoints Valkyrie queen of Asgard and joins the Guardians of the Galaxy. After returning the Infinity Stones and Thor's hammer to their original timelines, Rogers chooses to travel back and live his life in the past with Carter. He arrives as an elderly man and passes his shield onto Sam Wilson.

I just watched the movie, and Im speechless... 
In a weird way....
I feel empty cause I waited for so long for a perfect movie which I realize now that the perfection will always be foreshadowed by the existence of an ending, or if you want...perfection doesn't mean happiness and excitement, 
I feel empty cause I was sitting for 3 hours in that theatre and not even a minute seemed to pass...but as the credits started to roll, time started as well...
I feel empty cause I don't know what to feel when I am so sad and happy at the same time. There isn't a clear feeling, just a confusion, a positive confusion...
Sometimes scenes don't have to be unexpected to be awesome, they just have to deliver with the best way the expected...
Something like that maybe is just a reminder of the reality, from which there no escape...
-It isn't the ending that we wanted, but the ending that we needed

I can't believe that this era is ending. I love every character in the MCU. They all are so perfect in their own way. Marvel's every movie makes me love them even more. When I first started watching the films, I was really impressed, but I couldn't think that they would really affect me. Well, I was soooo wrong! Every movie that Marvel Cinematic Universe made shocked me. I can't express myself very good, but every time I watch this trailer I cry. I watched every MCU movie and know every one of these characters. One day I will join the MCU squad. That's my dream and I will try my best. How can every movie have the best script, the best cinematography, the best actors, the best CGI... Marvel made my life better in every way, I became more creative and happy. These heroes inspired me, even though it might sound lame.I can't believe that this movie will end it all. My personal favorite original story is however Captain America's because he was no one at first. He only was a good man but, the way that the MCU made him more powerful that no one never had imagined, well that shocked me. Some people may find Marvel childish or just a CU, but I believe that Marvel is at the top of everything. Nothing they do would ever change the way I think about them, Marvel gave me a light of hope to follow my dreams. This friday I am going to watch the End Game, but I am not ready. I watched every MCU movie very delicately and nearly every movie made me sad and emotional, but this. This movie will end everything. A new era will start and some of the characters will die, but I don't want to face the music. I know that some people think that I may be overreacting, but the way I see this is nothing. Can anyone believe that we may not be seeing some of the characters. I mean, I was so familiar with them because in every movie they were there. I hope that the new era will be good and I know that, but nothing will be more good than the old one.I can remember the first Marvel that I have ever watched. It was Iron Man, when I finished it, I was like wow somethings in the future are going to happen! and I was right. The first time Tony Stark became Iron Man, I was so excited. Then I saw Captain America, then I knew that this cinematic universe would changed my life. Everyday I wake up and think about to how to improve myself. These heroes actually inspired me and I'm not even kidding. I have waited for sooo long for this movie and it is clear that I'm not ready. I watched the interviews of the the End Game work premier and I was like: This is the last time we are seeing them together in from of the cameras. And I started crying, I just love them soooooooo muchhhh!!! I hope that in the future they will not loose their friendship. Sadly, I got a spoiler from my friend and when I heard it I was going to faint. So, please DO NOT SPOIL END GAME! MCU is one of the best thing that I have ever did. Everything about them is perfect. This movie will shock the world to the ground, no one will ever digest it. End Game will change the world. I hope that it will get an oscar so that we can remember them one more time. I really want to write for forever, and I would. But I need to stop somewhere. I just wanted to thank Marvel to inspiring me too deeply. I love every movie that you made and appreciate it. I wouldn't mind if the movie was 100 hours long and I wouldn't loose my focus at all. I hope that I will go to a university which I will study acting, directing... and then, I will join this family. It has been a real honor watching every movie that these guys made, I really want to thank. As some of you imagined right now, I am drowning in my own tears. Iron Man, Captain America, Black Widow, Thor, Hulk, Black Phanter, Spider Man, Loki, Hawkeye, Captain Marvel, Doctor Strange, Vision... You all made me a better person, every one of them shocked me and inspired me with their own original stories. Just, thank you for everything, I love you. I wrote this before watching End Game, so you could imagine after. I will probably have a heart attack, I missed them so much because it has been so long before Infinity War (I didn't say Captain Marvel because they were only half a minute there).I will do everything I can to not get any spoilers. Thank you a million times MCU for making and shooting these films soooo professionally. I can't think a day without these heroes, even one missing. But here we go, thank  you for every single movie MCU.
-Selin Tekin

I am from one of the lucky countries that already premiered the Endgame.
So here is the plot.

1st scene was hawkeye teaching his daughter archery, then his daughter was snapped.
Next scene, was tony filming himself like in the trailer, captain marvel arrives, carries the ship otw to earth where pepper  pots and the others fetch them. 
Next scene.
They've figured thanos's place and gone to that with cao marvel, only learning that thanos used the stones to destroy the stones. Thor then sliced the head of thanos with the storm breaker.
Next scene 
A timeskip 5 years later, a rat comes to  Van, accidentally presses the return button so antman, was brought back from quantum realm.
Scott checks his daughter, and she's pretty big now, he had gone to the avengers base, talk about that the 5 years in earth, was just 5 hrs for him in the quantum realm, and tells them that  they can manipulate that boom, fetch the stones and resnap! They told that to stark, some scenes happen then stark figures out how to manipulate the quantum.
They've gone back in time, fetch the stones.
Some funny and intense scenes,the soul stone was gotten through black widow suddenly sacrificing herself, btw ancient one said that strange was supposed to be the Strongest Sorcerer Supreme so knowing that giving the time stone was strange's decision, she also gaved it. Then they went back, hulk snapped, and bring the half back but because of nebula's memory projector thing,  2014 thanos has gotten aware of the situation, he have went to the present with his whole army, then attacked the avengers's base. Some Ultra Hyped fight scenes like Fatfuck thor having the mjolnir and stormbreaker, and Captain America being worthy and using the mjolnir, tony using the stones again to turn thanos and his army to ashes. Tony died. Btw there are no post credit scenes.

Cinematography and VFX is very much in par with the expectation.*

*Some great fight scenes which as a fan I have always wished to see. They have really done a great job setting each character on the perfect canvas they needed to be portrayed. This is a very emotional movie too. The best crafted film in the marvel universe. The Russo brothers are pure epitome of perfection to showcase MCU at its best. Thank you Marvel Studios and Stan Lee for giving us such epicness.* 😊

Age of Ultron is a warning to society: don't rely too much on artificial intelligence.

Age of Ultron shows us that our society is dominated by technology, not the other way around.
The main conflict in Age of Ultron is about more power than transformation; power of who gets to decide the fate of humanity.
Superheroes are not necessarily moral pillars of the community.
Being a team and sticking together applies to our own lives.
The metaphor of a family does not neatly apply to a team of superheroes.

The Avengers (2012): Themes
Identity
Good vs. Evil
Competition
Justice and Judgment
Power
Perseverance
*fits*
Great music if really fits I think this movie will be great.

The characters' personal identities are separate and distant from their heroic identities.

The characters' heroic identities are just an enhanced variation of who they always are.

Good and evil exist within each of the characters as well as in their external conflicts.

Each character is essentially good or essentially evil, and nothing can change that.

Competition helps the Avengers settle into their individual roles.

Competition is a detriment to the Avengers and needs to be overcome.

Justice is in the eye of the beholder, and each character pursues his or her own notion of what it means.

Justice is an unchanging idea, and characters with a less than just attitude need to discover it here.

Power corrupts, and even good guys need to use it carefully lest it corrupt them.

Power is neither good nor evil. It solely depends on who uses it and why.

Perseverance is ultimately the only thing that saves the world, and the one thing the heroes need to learn to respect.

Perseverance is merely a mean to an end, not a virtue in and of itself.
Motivations and actions are unusually believable for the genre. Characters frequently make sacrifices, act to protect each other and in others' best interests.

Heroism, courage, self-sacrifice are front and center in most Marvel movies, but with stakes feeling even greater than usual here, they really stand out: along with teamwork. Several characters face ultimate test; most pass. Several make extremely difficult decisions while acting out of love. The crisis unifies the heroes instantly, reuniting factions.

The Avengers are best when they're working toward the same goal instead of letting others tear them apart.

Though they have their quirks and frailties, ultimately the Avengers all show courage can work to overcome their individual challenges so they can accomplish their greater mission. They're not always successful, but when it counts, they come through.

Teamwork and loyalty matter: united we stand, divided we fall. Don't let your fears get the better of you.

The Avengers are all fighting for justice and to overthrow evil. Each has quirks and frailties, but ultimately they overcome their individual challenges to accomplish their greater mission.
Not everyone in the good guys is always entirely on the up and up, the heroes do have their share of conflicts before they come together.
You can step up and be a hero, no matter what mistakes you might have made in the past.
Avengers Endgame is dedicated to the loving memory of Stan Lee (1922-2018)
